poem.content:
I lend my ear to fiction, spun of night,
where stars are false and shadows bleed like fire.
This rifle script, a future caught in flight,
a family choked, fulfilling dark desire.

I learn to swim against the churning tide,
to clarify the murk that hides the truth.
A year has passed, and still the pain won't hide,
a constant echo from my wasted youth.

A kind hand reaches, I can not receive,
the darkness clings, possessive and unkind.
I watch the ocean, and I start to grieve,
for all the things I left so far behind.

The target shifts, a phantom in the haze,
a fishing boat upon the distant shore.
A false promise in these sunlit days,
I learn to swim, and I can only implore,
the fire inside will simply cease to be,
and clarify a path for only me.
